sections.text: This Act may be cited as the Superior Courts (Number of Judges) Act, 2025.

In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires— “ Chief Justice ” means the Chief Justice appointed in accordance with Article 140 of the Constitution; [Cap. 1] “ Constitutional Court ” means the Constitutional Court established under Article 127 of the Constitution; [Cap. 1] “ Court of Appeal ” means the Court of Appeal established under Article 130 of the Constitution; [Cap. 1] “ High Court ” means the High Court established in accordance with Article 133 of the Constitution; [Cap. 1] “ Deputy Chief Justice ” means the Deputy Chief Justice appointed in accordance with Article 140 of the Constitution; [Cap. 1] “ Deputy Judge President ” has the meaning assigned to the words in the Court of Appeal Act, 2016; [ Act No. 7 of 2016 ] “ Deputy President of the Constitutional Court ” means the Deputy President of the Constitution Court appointed under Article 140 of the Constitution; [Cap. 1] “ judge ” has the meaning assigned to the word in the Constitution and the word “judges” shall be construed accordingly; [Cap. 1] “ Judge President ” has the meaning assigned to the words in the Court of Appeal Act, 2016; [ Act No. 7 of 2016 ] “ superior court ” has the meaning assigned to the words in the Constitution; and [Cap. 1] " Supreme Court " means the Supreme Court established under Article 124 of the Constitution; [Cap. 1]
There shall be thirteen judges of the Supreme Court , including the Chief Justice and the Deputy Chief Justice .
There shall be thirteen judges of the Constitutional Court , including the President of the Constitutional Court and the Deputy President of the Constitutional Court .
There shall be not more than thirty-one judges of the Court of Appeal , including the Judge President and the Deputy Judge President .
There shall be not more than one hundred judges of the High Court .
The Superior Courts (Number of Judges) Act, 2016, is repealed.
